human_task_templates.name: QualiTrace_AI_HumanTaskTemplates
human_task_templates.description: Defines all 5 human task templates for QualiTrace AI. Every template assigns to quality_lead, exposes confidence_score and source_list, provides approve/reject/override buttons, and requires override_reason when decision is override. All field names are exact snake_case from CaseDefinition.json.

global_template_rules.note: These rules apply to all 5 templates below. Individual templates may add display fields and context beyond the minimum — they may not remove confidence_score, source_list, or the override_reason requirement.

templates.description: Triggered when batch_number is null at any stage. The case is paused at status = pending_review and cannot advance until the reviewer supplies the missing batch_number. This task does not ask for an approve/reject decision on risk — it asks the reviewer to locate and enter the missing information so routing can resume.

display_fields.label: Case ID
display_fields.description: Unique identifier for this complaint case.

display_fields.label: Product Name
display_fields.description: Name of the pharmaceutical product involved.

display_fields.label: Batch Number
display_fields.description: The batch number is missing. This field must be supplied before the case can continue.

display_fields.label: Complaint Type
display_fields.description: Category of the reported complaint.

display_fields.label: Jurisdiction
display_fields.description: Regulatory jurisdiction — US or EU.

display_fields.label: Risk Level
display_fields.description: Risk classification assigned at intake.

display_fields.label: AI Confidence Score
display_fields.description: AI confidence in the current classification. Displayed to reviewer for context even during a pause.

display_fields.label: Supporting Citations
display_fields.description: Document sources retrieved by the RAG pipeline. Displayed so the reviewer understands what the AI has already found.

display_fields.label: Case Status
display_fields.description: Case is paused pending missing information.

input_fields.label: Enter Batch Number
input_fields.description: Reviewer must enter the correct batch number to unblock routing. Entered value is saved to the case record and triggers resume.

action_buttons.label: Submit Batch Number & Resume
action_buttons.description: Submits the entered batch_number, updates the case record, sets status back to open, and resumes routing from the paused stage.

action_buttons.label: Escalate — Cannot Locate Batch
action_buttons.description: Used when the batch number cannot be found. Escalates to quality_lead for further investigation. Case remains in pending_review.

action_buttons.label: Override — Proceed Without Batch Number
action_buttons.description: Allows the case to proceed without a batch number under exceptional circumstances. override_reason is mandatory.

sla_reference.note: SLA for missing_info tasks is driven by the risk_level of the paused case. See SLAConfig.json task_type_overrides.missing_info for time limits.

audit_events: TASK_CREATED: missing_info task opened, case paused
TASK_RESOLVED: batch_number supplied, routing resumed
TASK_ESCALATED: batch number not found
HUMAN_OVERRIDE: proceeded without batch_number, override_reason recorded

templates.description: Triggered when confidence_score < 0.6 at Stage 2 or Stage 3. The AI is not sufficiently confident in its risk classification or investigation findings. The reviewer sees the full AI output and must make a formal decision before the case can advance.

display_fields.label: AI-Assigned Risk Level
display_fields.description: Risk level assigned by the Intake Agent. Reviewer may override this assessment.

display_fields.label: AI Confidence Score
display_fields.description: Score is below 0.6 — this is why human review was triggered. Displayed prominently.

display_fields.label: Supporting Citations
display_fields.description: All document sources the AI used to reach its conclusion. Reviewer should assess citation quality when making their decision.

display_fields.label: AI Recommendation
display_fields.description: The AI's risk classification and reasoning summary. Presented before the reviewer makes a decision.

display_fields.label: Root Cause Hypotheses
display_fields.description: Up to 3 ranked hypotheses from the Investigation Agent, each with its own confidence and source citations.

action_buttons.label: Approve AI Assessment
action_buttons.description: Reviewer agrees with the AI risk classification and findings. Case advances to Stage 5 CAPA. human_approval_flag is set to true.

action_buttons.label: Reject — Return for Re-Investigation
action_buttons.description: Reviewer disagrees with the AI findings. Case routes back to Stage 3 AutoInvestigation for a fresh run.

action_buttons.label: Override AI — Enter Own Assessment
action_buttons.description: Reviewer replaces the AI assessment with their own. override_reason is mandatory and is recorded in the audit trail.
side_effects: override_reason written to case record and audit trail

sla_reference.note: SLA is set by the risk_level of the case at time of task creation.

audit_events: TASK_CREATED: risk_review task opened — low confidence triggered
HUMAN_APPROVE: reviewer approved AI assessment
HUMAN_REJECT: reviewer rejected — re-investigation required
HUMAN_OVERRIDE: reviewer overrode AI, override_reason recorded

templates.description: Triggered after the CAPA Agent generates a corrective and preventive action plan. The reviewer assesses the plan for completeness, citation coverage, and regulatory appropriateness before it is actioned. Also used when CAPA generation fails after 3 retries and a human must step in.

display_fields.label: Jurisdiction
display_fields.description: CAPA actions are jurisdiction-specific. FDA requirements differ from EMA requirements.

display_fields.label: Risk Level
display_fields.description: Risk level at time of CAPA generation.

display_fields.label: AI Confidence Score
display_fields.description: Confidence from the investigation stage that underpins this CAPA plan.

display_fields.label: Cited Regulatory Sources
display_fields.description: Every CAPA action must be traceable to at least one source in this list. Reviewer should verify citation coverage.

display_fields.label: Approved Root Cause
display_fields.description: The root cause hypothesis that was approved in Stage 4 HumanReview. The CAPA plan should directly address this.

display_fields.label: CAPA Action Items
display_fields.description: Full list of corrective and preventive actions generated by the CAPA Agent.

action_buttons.label: Approve CAPA Plan
action_buttons.description: Reviewer approves the CAPA plan as presented. All actions are accepted and will be actioned. Case advances to Stage 6 EffectivenessReview.

action_buttons.label: Reject — Revise CAPA Plan
action_buttons.description: Reviewer finds the CAPA plan inadequate. Case routes back to Stage 5 for CAPA revision.
side_effects: CAPA_REVISION_REQUIRED event written to audit trail

action_buttons.label: Override — Modify and Approve
action_buttons.description: Reviewer approves with modifications. override_reason must document what was changed and why.
side_effects: HUMAN_OVERRIDE event written to audit trail

sla_reference.note: CAPA approval tasks have a fixed SLA of 48 hours regardless of risk_level. See SLAConfig.json.

audit_events: TASK_CREATED: capa_approval task opened
HUMAN_APPROVE: CAPA plan approved
HUMAN_REJECT: CAPA plan rejected — revision required
HUMAN_OVERRIDE: CAPA plan modified and approved, override_reason recorded

templates.display_name: Critical Escalation — Immediate Human Review Required
templates.description: Triggered when risk_level == 'critical' at Stage 2. The case bypasses AutoInvestigation entirely and arrives directly at this task. Also triggered on SLA breach for any critical-risk case. This is the highest-priority task in the system — 4-hour SLA from task creation.

display_fields.label: Batch Number
display_fields.description: Batch number for immediate hold consideration.

display_fields.label: Jurisdiction
display_fields.description: Determines applicable regulatory reporting obligations — FDA (US) or EMA (EU).

display_fields.label: Risk Level
display_fields.description: This case has been classified as CRITICAL. Immediate human review is mandatory before any investigation begins.

display_fields.label: AI Confidence Score
display_fields.description: AI confidence in the critical classification. Even at high confidence, human review is mandatory for all critical cases.

display_fields.label: Supporting Citations
display_fields.description: Regulatory sources retrieved to support the critical classification.

display_fields.label: AI Risk Assessment
display_fields.description: The AI's full critical risk reasoning, including patient impact assessment.

display_fields.label: SLA Deadline
display_fields.description: Critical cases must be reviewed within 4 hours of task creation. SLA timer is active.

display_fields.label: Batch Hold Status
display_fields.description: Indicates whether BatchHoldSimulator.xaml has been triggered to place a hold on the batch in the ERP system.

action_buttons.label: Confirm Critical — Proceed to Investigation
action_buttons.description: Reviewer confirms the critical classification. Case proceeds to Stage 3 AutoInvestigation (unlike normal critical routing, this explicit human confirmation allows investigation to begin). human_approval_flag is set to true.
side_effects: batch_hold_triggered if batch_number is not null

action_buttons.label: Downgrade — Return for Re-Classification
action_buttons.description: Reviewer determines the case does not warrant critical classification. Case routes back to Stage 2 RiskAssessment for re-evaluation.
side_effects: CRITICAL_DOWNGRADE event written to audit trail

action_buttons.label: Override Classification — Enter Revised Assessment
action_buttons.description: Reviewer replaces the AI's critical classification with a different risk_level or course of action. override_reason is mandatory and must document the clinical or regulatory basis for the override.

sla_reference.note: Critical escalation tasks carry the strictest SLA in the system — 4 hours from task creation. SLA warning fires at 75% (3 hours). Breach escalates immediately to quality_lead.

escalation_on_sla_breach.audit_event: SLA_BREACH: critical_escalation task overdue — escalated to quality_lead

rpa_integrations.description: Places a simulated batch hold in the mock ERP system as soon as a critical_escalation task is created.

audit_events: TASK_CREATED: critical_escalation task opened — risk_level critical
BATCH_HOLD_TRIGGERED: batch hold placed via BatchHoldSimulator.xaml
HUMAN_APPROVE: critical classification confirmed — investigation authorised
CRITICAL_DOWNGRADE: reviewer downgraded from critical — re-classification required
HUMAN_OVERRIDE: critical classification overridden, override_reason recorded
SLA_BREACH: 4-hour SLA exceeded — quality_lead notified

templates.description: Triggered before Stage 7 Closure. This is the mandatory final human approval gate. No case may be closed without a formal decision recorded by this task. It provides the human_approval_flag = true that Stage 7 requires as a hard entry condition. The reviewer sees a complete case summary before signing off.

trigger.gates: Stage7_Closure entry is blocked until this task resolves with approve or override

display_fields.label: Final AI Confidence Score
display_fields.description: The confidence score from the investigation phase. Shown as part of the complete case record.

display_fields.label: All Cited Sources
display_fields.description: Complete list of regulatory and SOP sources cited across the entire case lifecycle.

display_fields.label: Approved Root Cause
display_fields.description: The root cause confirmed in Stage 4.

display_fields.label: Completed CAPA Actions
display_fields.description: All CAPA actions with their completion status. All must be complete before closure is permitted.

display_fields.label: Effectiveness Review Result
display_fields.description: Confirmed pass from Stage 6 EffectivenessReview.

display_fields.label: Override Reason (if applicable)
display_fields.description: Any override_reason recorded during the case lifecycle. Surfaced here for the reviewer's awareness before sign-off.

display_fields.label: Human Approval Status
display_fields.description: Will be set to true upon approval or override. Stage 7 Closure is hard-blocked until this is true.

display_fields.label: Current Case Status
display_fields.description: Will transition to closed upon approval.

display_fields.label: Case Audit Trail
display_fields.description: Complete timeline of all events, decisions, and transitions across the entire case lifecycle.

action_buttons.label: Approve Closure
action_buttons.description: Reviewer formally approves case closure. human_approval_flag is set to true. Stage 7 entry is unblocked. Case status transitions to closed.
side_effects: ReportGenerator.xaml triggered to produce closure PDF
CASE_CLOSED event written to audit trail

action_buttons.label: Reject Closure — Reopen Case
action_buttons.description: Reviewer determines the case is not ready for closure — for example, CAPA evidence is insufficient. Case is reopened and routed back to Stage 6 EffectivenessReview.
side_effects: CLOSURE_REJECTED event written to audit trail

action_buttons.label: Override — Close with Conditions
action_buttons.description: Reviewer closes the case with noted conditions or reservations. override_reason must document the conditions under which closure is being approved.
side_effects: HUMAN_OVERRIDE — CONDITIONAL_CLOSURE event written to audit trail

sla_reference.note: Final closure tasks inherit the SLA of the case's risk_level. See SLAConfig.json.

hard_gate.description: This task is the mandatory gate for Stage 7 entry. Stage 7 Closure checks human_approval_flag == true as a hard entry condition. If this task resolves with reject, human_approval_flag remains false and Stage 7 is blocked.

rpa_integrations.description: Generates and saves the final closure PDF report for the case.

audit_events: TASK_CREATED: final_closure task opened
HUMAN_APPROVE: closure approved — human_approval_flag set true
CLOSURE_REJECTED: reviewer rejected closure — case reopened to Stage 6
HUMAN_OVERRIDE: conditional closure approved, override_reason recorded
CASE_CLOSED: status set to closed, closure report generated

field_references.note: All field names below are exact snake_case identifiers from CaseDefinition.json. No aliases or camelCase equivalents are used anywhere in this file.
